title: hieracium valdepilosum subsp. suillaeviae
intro: Hieracium valdepilosum subsp. suillaeviae is a lesser-known species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), often referred to as a type of hawkweed. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections, due to its potential health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
